channel.title: Lantern Tides devlog
channel.description: Notes from building a small narrative game, frame by frame, by Angela Brooks.

item.title: Build 0.6.5, the night sky gets a soft wall
item.description: The chapter four edge that kept crashing is now gated behind a soft wall, so the demo never reaches the unfinished tiles. Chapters one through three stay clean. Next up is the letter chapter font pass.

item.title: The river road monologue, and why I underplay it
item.description: Testers keep naming the graveyard scene the day after. Nobody names a mechanic. That is the whole thesis in one playtest. A game about attention should be remembered for the quiet beats.

item.title: Build 0.6.3, the bridge softlock is dead
item.description: The lantern puzzle on the river road bridge could strand the player. Now the lantern state resets when you step off the bridge tile. Two sessions hit it before the fix, zero after.

item.title: Palette study, the lantern glow color
item.description: Three hours on two hex values, and I would do it again. The lantern core sits one step warm of the rim so the bloom reads at one pixel size. Small craft is the work, not the polish.

item.title: Showcase date locked, November 8
item.description: The playable demo targets the Cascadia Indie Showcase on November 8, 2026. Scope is chapters one through three polished, plus a chapter four teaser. Ravi takes the audio branch from here.
